scenario: Meridian Insurance rolls out Camunda 8.8 Tasklist to 350 users who handle 6000 tasks per day across claims triage and underwriting. The product owner is preparing onboarding videos and wants a one-line summary of the top-level pages an end user sees in Tasklist, so the materials match the navigation that ships in the Camunda 8.8 release.
question: Which two main pages does Tasklist present to an end user in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The Tasks page lists tasks available to the user or group, and the Processes page lets the user start new processes from deployed definitions
The Inbox page lists tasks claimed by the user, and the Outbox page lists tasks the user has completed during the latest reporting period
The Operations page lists running process instances, and the Tasks page lets the user create ad-hoc tasks for colleagues across the cluster
The Dashboard page summarizes assigned tasks, and the Search page lets the user run free-text queries across the historical task archive

a.text: Correct. The Tasklist user guide names two main pages: a Tasks page to manage tasks and a Processes page to start processes. End users navigate between exactly these two top-level views.

b.text: Incorrect. Tasklist does not expose Inbox and Outbox pages. The two documented main pages are the Tasks page (manage tasks) and the Processes page (start processes).

c.text: Incorrect. Browsing running process instances is an Operate concern. Tasklist's two main pages per the user guide are the Tasks page and the Processes page.

d.text: Incorrect. Tasklist has no Dashboard or Search page at the top level. The user guide documents exactly two main pages: Tasks and Processes.

explanation: Tasklist's user guide lists two main top-level pages: the Tasks page, used to manage tasks available to the user or group, and the Processes page, used to start new processes from deployed definitions.
